title: Treasury Moves to Prevent Abuse of Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFI) Fund Programs
summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury announced on April 9, 2026, that the CDFI Fund will issue rules to ensure CDFI awards comply with the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996 (PRWORA), specifically to prevent illegal aliens from receiving taxpayer-funded federal public benefits through CDFI programs. Treasury also announced anti-discrimination compliance reforms for certified CDFIs.
key_points: Treasury announced the CDFI Fund will issue rules clarifying that certain benefits are 'federal public benefits' under PRWORA, making illegal aliens ineligible for these funds.
Secretary Scott Bessent stated Treasury will use its authority to prevent waste, fraud, and abuse in the CDFI Fund.
New provisions will be added to CDFI Fund agreements requiring certified CDFIs to comply with federal anti-discrimination laws.
Certified CDFIs must adopt policies against discrimination based on race, ethnicity, or sex inconsistent with federal law.
CDFIs must annually certify compliance and make policies available for CDFI Fund review upon request.
Non-compliance consequences include potential decertification, termination of unused funds, and recapture of past award funds.
why_it_matters: This is a regulatory/policy announcement affecting Community Development Financial Institutions. It does not relate to financial market structure or macro catalysts such as monetary policy, interest rates, or economic data releases.
